chapters.en: Then Paul, after that the governor had beckoned unto him to speak, answered, Forasmuch as I know that thou hast been of many years a judge unto this nation, I do the more cheerfully answer for myself:

chapters.zh: 你查问就可以知道，从我上耶路撒冷礼拜到今日不过有十二天。
chapters.en: Because that thou mayest understand, that there are yet but twelve days since I went up to Jerusalem for to worship.

chapters.zh: 他们并没有看见我在殿里，或是在会堂里，或是在城里，和人辩论，耸动众人。
chapters.en: And they neither found me in the temple disputing with any man, neither raising up the people, neither in the synagogues, nor in the city:

chapters.zh: 他们现在所告我的事并不能对你证实了。
chapters.en: Neither can they prove the things whereof they now accuse me.

chapters.zh: 但有一件事，我向你承认，就是他们所称为异端的道，我正按著那道事奉我祖宗的神，又信合乎律法的和先知书上一切所记载的，
chapters.en: But this I confess unto thee, that after the way which they call heresy, so worship I the God of my fathers, believing all things which are written in the law and in the prophets:

chapters.zh: 并且靠著神，盼望死人，无论善恶，都要复活，就是他们自己也有这个盼望。
chapters.en: And have hope toward God, which they themselves also allow, that there shall be a resurrection of the dead, both of the just and unjust.

chapters.zh: 我因此自己勉励，对神对人，常存无亏的良心。
chapters.en: And herein do I exercise myself, to have always a conscience void of offence toward God, and men.

chapters.zh: 过了几年，我带著赒济本国的捐项和供献的物上去。
chapters.en: Now after many years I came to bring alms to my nation, and offerings.

chapters.zh: 正献的时候，他们看见我在殿里已经洁净了，并没有聚众，也没有吵嚷，惟有几个从亚西亚来的犹太人。
chapters.en: Whereupon certain Jews from Asia found me purified in the temple, neither with multitude, nor with tumult.

chapters.zh: 他们若有告我的事，就应当到你面前来告我。
chapters.en: Who ought to have been here before thee, and object, if they had ought against me.

chapters.zh: 即或不然，这些人若看出我站在公会前，有妄为的地方，他们自己也可以说明。
chapters.en: Or else let these same say, if they have found any evil doing in me, while I stood before the council,

chapters.zh: 纵然有，也不过一句话，就是我站在他们中间大声说：我今日在你们面前受审，是为死人复活的道理。
chapters.en: Except it be for this one voice, that I cried standing among them, Touching the resurrection of the dead I am called in question by you this day.

chapters.zh: 腓力斯本是详细晓得这道，就支吾他们说：且等千夫长吕西亚下来，我要审断你们的事。
chapters.en: And when Felix heard these things, having more perfect knowledge of way, he deferred them, and said, When Lysias the chief captain shall come down, I will know the uttermost of your matter.

chapters.zh: 于是吩咐百夫长看守保罗，并且宽待他，也不拦阻他的亲友来供给他。
chapters.en: And he commanded a centurion to keep Paul, and to let have liberty, and that he should forbid none of his acquaintance to minister or come unto him.

chapters.zh: 过了几天，腓力斯和他夫人 ─ 犹太的女子土西拉 ─ 一同来到，就叫了保罗来，听他讲论信基督耶稣的道。
chapters.en: And after certain days, when Felix came with his wife Drusilla, which was a Jewess, he sent for Paul, and heard him concerning the faith in Christ.

chapters.zh: 保罗讲论公义、节制，和将来的审判。腓力斯甚觉恐惧，说：你暂且去罢，等我得便再叫你来。保罗讲论公义、节制，和将来的审判。腓力斯甚觉恐惧，说：你暂且去罢，等我得便再叫你来。
chapters.en: And as he reasoned of righteousness, temperance, and judgment to come, Felix trembled, and answered, Go thy way for this time; when I have a convenient season, I will call for thee.

chapters.zh: 腓力斯又指望保罗送他银钱，所以屡次叫他来，和他谈论。
chapters.en: He hoped also that money should have been given him of Paul, that he might loose him: wherefore he sent for him the oftener, and communed with him.

chapters.zh: 过了两年，波求非斯都接了腓力斯的任；腓力斯要讨犹太人的喜欢，就留保罗在监里。
chapters.en: But after two years Porcius Festus came into Felix' room: and Felix, willing to shew the Jews a pleasure, left Paul bound.

chapters.zh: 非斯都到了任，过了三天，就从该撒利亚上耶路撒冷去。
chapters.en: Now when Festus was come into the province, after three days he ascended from Caesarea to Jerusalem.

chapters.zh: 祭司长和犹太人的首领向他控告保罗，
chapters.en: Then the high priest and the chief of the Jews informed him against Paul, and besought him,

chapters.zh: 又央告他，求他的情，将保罗提到耶路撒冷来，他们要在路上埋伏杀害他。
chapters.en: And desired favour against him, that he would send for him to Jerusalem, laying wait in the way to kill him.

chapters.zh: 非斯都却回答说：保罗押在该撒利亚，我自己快要往那里去；
chapters.en: But Festus answered, that Paul should be kept at Caesarea, and that he himself would depart shortly .

chapters.zh: 又说：你们中间有权势的人与我一同下去，那人若有甚么不是，就可以告他。
chapters.en: Let them therefore, said he, which among you are able, go down with , and accuse this man, if there be any wickedness in him.

chapters.zh: 非斯都在他们那里住了不过十天八天，就下该撒利亚去；第二天坐堂，吩咐将保罗提上来。
chapters.en: And when he had tarried among them more than ten days, he went down unto Caesarea; and the next day sitting on the judgment seat commanded Paul to be brought.

chapters.zh: 保罗来了，那些从耶路撒冷下来的犹太人周围站著，将许多重大的事控告他，都是不能证实的。
chapters.en: And when he was come, the Jews which came down from Jerusalem stood round about, and laid many and grievous complaints against Paul, which they could not prove.

chapters.zh: 保罗分诉说：无论犹太人的律法，或是圣殿，或是该撒，我都没有干犯。
chapters.en: While he answered for himself, Neither against the law of the Jews, neither against the temple, nor yet against Caesar, have I offended any thing at all.

chapters.zh: 但非斯都要讨犹太人的喜欢，就问保罗说：你愿意上耶路撒冷去，在那里听我审断这事么？
chapters.en: But Festus, willing to do the Jews a pleasure, answered Paul, and said, Wilt thou go up to Jerusalem, and there be judged of these things before me?

chapters.zh: 保罗说：我站在该撒的堂前，这就是我应当受审的地方。我向犹太人并没有行过甚么不义的事，这也是你明明知道的。
chapters.en: Then said Paul, I stand at Caesar's judgment seat, where I ought to be judged: to the Jews have I done no wrong, as thou very well knowest.

chapters.zh: 我若行了不义的事，犯了甚么该死的罪，就是死，我也不辞。他们所告我的事若都不实，就没有人可以把我交给他们。我要上告于该撒。
chapters.en: For if I be an offender, or have committed any thing worthy of death, I refuse not to die: but if there be none of these things whereof these accuse me, no man may deliver me unto them. I appeal unto Caesar.

chapters.zh: 非斯都和议会商量了，就说：你既上告于该撒，可以往该撒那里去。
chapters.en: Then Festus, when he had conferred with the council, answered, Hast thou appealed unto Caesar? unto Caesar shalt thou go.

chapters.zh: 过了些日子，亚基帕王和百尼基氏来到该撒利亚，问非斯都安。
chapters.en: And after certain days king Agrippa and Bernice came unto Caesarea to salute Festus.

chapters.zh: 在那里住了多日，非斯都将保罗的事告诉王，说：这里有一个人，是腓力斯留在监里的。
chapters.en: And when they had been there many days, Festus declared Paul's cause unto the king, saying, There is a certain man left in bonds by Felix:

chapters.zh: 我在耶路撒冷的时候，祭司长和犹太的长老将他的事禀报了我，求我定他的罪。
chapters.en: About whom, when I was at Jerusalem, the chief priests and the elders of the Jews informed , desiring judgment against him.

chapters.zh: 我对他们说，无论甚么人，被告还没有和原告对质，未得机会分诉所告他的事，就先定他的罪，这不是罗马人的条例。
chapters.en: To whom I answered, It is not the manner of the Romans to deliver any man to die, before that he which is accused have the accusers face to face, and have licence to answer for himself concerning the crime laid against him.

chapters.zh: 及至他们都来到这里，我就不耽延，第二天便坐堂，吩咐把那人提上来。
chapters.en: Therefore, when they were come hither, without any delay on the morrow I sat on the judgment seat, and commanded the man to be brought forth.

chapters.zh: 告他的人站著告他；所告的，并没有我所逆料的那等恶事。
chapters.en: Against whom when the accusers stood up, they brought none accusation of such things as I supposed:

chapters.zh: 不过是有几样辩论，为他们自己敬鬼神的事，又为一个人名叫耶稣，是已经死了，保罗却说他是活著的。
chapters.en: But had certain questions against him of their own superstition, and of one Jesus, which was dead, whom Paul affirmed to be alive.

chapters.zh: 这些事当怎样究问，我心里作难，所以问他说：你愿意上耶路撒冷去，在那里为这些事听审么？
chapters.en: And because I doubted of such manner of questions, I asked whether he would go to Jerusalem, and there be judged of these matters.

chapters.zh: 但保罗求我留下他，要听皇上审断，我就吩咐把他留下，等我解他到该撒那里去。
chapters.en: But when Paul had appealed to be reserved unto the hearing of Augustus, I commanded him to be kept till I might send him to Caesar.

chapters.zh: 亚基帕对非斯都说：我自己也愿听这人辩论。非斯都说：明天你可以听。
chapters.en: Then Agrippa said unto Festus, I would also hear the man myself. To morrow, said he, thou shalt hear him.

chapters.zh: 第二天，亚基帕和百尼基大张威势而来，同著众千夫长和城里的尊贵人进了公厅。非斯都吩咐一声，就有人将保罗带进来。
chapters.en: And on the morrow, when Agrippa was come, and Bernice, with great pomp, and was entered into the place of hearing, with the chief captains, and principal men of the city, at Festus' commandment Paul was brought forth.

chapters.zh: 非斯都说：亚基帕王和在这里的诸位阿，你们看这人，就是一切犹太人，在耶路撒冷和这里，曾向我恳求、呼叫说：不可容他再活著。
chapters.en: And Festus said, King Agrippa, and all men which are here present with us, ye see this man, about whom all the multitude of the Jews have dealt with me, both at Jerusalem, and here, crying that he ought not to live any longer.

chapters.zh: 但我查明他没有犯甚么该死的罪，并且他自己上告于皇帝，所以我定意把他解去。
chapters.en: But when I found that he had committed nothing worthy of death, and that he himself hath appealed to Augustus, I have determined to send him.

chapters.zh: 论到这人，我没有确实的事可以奏明主上。因此，我带他到你们面前，也特意带他到你亚基帕王面前，为要在查问之后有所陈奏。
chapters.en: Of whom I have no certain thing to write unto my lord. Wherefore I have brought him forth before you, and specially before thee, O king Agrippa, that, after examination had, I might have somewhat to write.

chapters.zh: 据我看来，解送囚犯，不指明他的罪案是不合理的。
chapters.en: For it seemeth to me unreasonable to send a prisoner, and not withal to signify the crimes against him.

chapters.zh: 亚基帕对保罗说：准你为自己辩明。于是保罗伸手分诉，说：
chapters.en: Then Agrippa said unto Paul, Thou art permitted to speak for thyself. Then Paul stretched forth the hand, and answered for himself:

chapters.zh: 亚基帕王阿，犹太人所告我的一切事，今日得在你面前分诉，实为万幸；
chapters.en: I think myself happy, king Agrippa, because I shall answer for myself this day before thee touching all the things whereof I am accused of the Jews:

chapters.zh: 更可幸的，是你熟悉犹太人的规矩和他们的辩论；所以求你耐心听我。
chapters.en: Especially thee to be expert in all customs and questions which are among the Jews: wherefore I beseech thee to hear me patiently.

chapters.zh: 我从起初在本国的民中，并在耶路撒冷，自幼为人如何，犹太人都知道。
chapters.en: My manner of life from my youth, which was at the first among mine own nation at Jerusalem, know all the Jews;

chapters.zh: 他们若肯作见证就晓得，我从起初是按著我们教中最严紧的教门作了法利赛人。
chapters.en: Which knew me from the beginning, if they would testify, that after the most straitest sect of our religion I lived a Pharisee.

chapters.zh: 现在我站在这里受审，是因为指望神向我们祖宗所应许的；
chapters.en: And now I stand and am judged for the hope of the promise made of God unto our fathers:

chapters.zh: 这应许，我们十二个支派，昼夜切切的事奉神，都指望得著。王阿，我被犹太人控告，就是因这指望。
chapters.en: Unto which our twelve tribes, instantly serving day and night, hope to come. For which hope's sake, king Agrippa, I am accused of the Jews.

chapters.zh: 神叫死人复活，你们为甚么看作不可信的呢？
chapters.en: Why should it be thought a thing incredible with you, that God should raise the dead?

chapters.zh: 从前我自己以为应当多方攻击拿撒勒人耶稣的名，
chapters.en: I verily thought with myself, that I ought to do many things contrary to the name of Jesus of Nazareth.

chapters.zh: 我在耶路撒冷也曾这样行了。既从祭司长得了权柄，我就把许多圣徒囚在监里。他们被杀，我也出名定案。
chapters.en: Which thing I also did in Jerusalem: and many of the saints did I shut up in prison, having received authority from the chief priests; and when they were put to death, I gave my voice against .

chapters.zh: 在各会堂，我屡次用刑强逼他们说亵渎的话，又分外恼恨他们，甚至追逼他们，直到外邦的城邑。
chapters.en: And I punished them oft in every synagogue, and compelled to blaspheme; and being exceedingly mad against them, I persecuted even unto strange cities.

chapters.zh: 那时，我领了祭司长的权柄和命令，往大马色去。
chapters.en: Whereupon as I went to Damascus with authority and commission from the chief priests,

chapters.zh: 王阿，我在路上，晌午的时候，看见从天发光，比日头还亮，四面照著我并与我同行的人。
chapters.en: At midday, O king, I saw in the way a light from heaven, above the brightness of the sun, shining round about me and them which journeyed with me.

chapters.zh: 我们都仆倒在地，我就听见有声音用希伯来话向我说：扫罗！扫罗！为甚么逼迫我？你用脚踢刺是难的！
chapters.en: And when we were all fallen to the earth, I heard a voice speaking unto me, and saying in the Hebrew tongue, Saul, Saul, why persecutest thou me? hard for thee to kick against the pricks.

chapters.zh: 我说：主阿，你是谁？主说：我就是你所逼迫的耶稣。
chapters.en: And I said, Who art thou, Lord? And he said, I am Jesus whom thou persecutest.

chapters.zh: 你起来站著，我特意向你显现，要派你作执事，作见证，将你所看见的事和我将要指示你的事证明出来；
chapters.en: But rise, and stand upon thy feet: for I have appeared unto thee for this purpose, to make thee a minister and a witness both of these things which thou hast seen, and of those things in the which I will appear unto thee;

chapters.zh: 我也要救你脱离百姓和外邦人的手。
chapters.en: Delivering thee from the people, and the Gentiles, unto whom now I send thee,

chapters.zh: 我差你到他们那里去，要叫他们的眼睛得开，从黑暗中归向光明，从撒但权下归向神；又因信我，得蒙赦罪，和一切成圣的人同得基业。
chapters.en: To open their eyes, to turn from darkness to light, and the power of Satan unto God, that they may receive forgiveness of sins, and inheritance among them which are sanctified by faith that is in me.

chapters.zh: 亚基帕王阿，我故此没有违背那从天上来的异象；
chapters.en: Whereupon, O king Agrippa, I was not disobedient unto the heavenly vision:

chapters.zh: 先在大马色，后在耶路撒冷和犹太全地，以及外邦，劝勉他们应当悔改归向神，行事与悔改的心相称。
chapters.en: But shewed first unto them of Damascus, and at Jerusalem, and throughout all the coasts of Judaea, and to the Gentiles, that they should repent and turn to God, and do works meet for repentance.

chapters.zh: 因此，犹太人在殿里拿住我，想要杀我。
chapters.en: For these causes the Jews caught me in the temple, and went about to kill .

chapters.zh: 然而我蒙神的帮助，直到今日还站得住，对著尊贵、卑贱、老幼作见证；所讲的并不外乎众先知和摩西所说将来必成的事，
chapters.en: Having therefore obtained help of God, I continue unto this day, witnessing both to small and great, saying none other things than those which the prophets and Moses did say should come:

chapters.zh: 就是基督必须受害，并且因从死里复活，要首先把光明的道传给百姓和外邦人。
chapters.en: That Christ should suffer, that he should be the first that should rise from the dead, and should shew light unto the people, and to the Gentiles.

chapters.zh: 保罗这样分诉，非斯都大声说：保罗，你癫狂了罢。你的学问太大，反叫你癫狂了！
chapters.en: And as he thus spake for himself, Festus said with a loud voice, Paul, thou art beside thyself; much learning doth make thee mad.

chapters.zh: 保罗说：非斯都大人，我不是癫狂，我说的乃是真实明白话。
chapters.en: But he said, I am not mad, most noble Festus; but speak forth the words of truth and soberness.

chapters.zh: 王也晓得这些事，所以我向王放胆直言，我深信这些事没有一件向王隐藏的，因都不是在背地里做的。
chapters.en: For the king knoweth of these things, before whom also I speak freely: for I am persuaded that none of these things are hidden from him; for this thing was not done in a corner.

chapters.zh: 亚基帕王阿，你信先知么？我知道你是信的。
chapters.en: King Agrippa, believest thou the prophets? I know that thou believest.

chapters.zh: 亚基帕对保罗说：你想少微一劝，便叫我作基督徒阿（或作：你这样劝我，几乎叫我作基督徒了）！
chapters.en: Then Agrippa said unto Paul, Almost thou persuadest me to be a Christian.

chapters.zh: 保罗说：无论是少劝是多劝，我向神所求的，不但你一个人，就是今天一切听我的，都要像我一样，只是不要像我有这些锁炼。
chapters.en: And Paul said, I would to God, that not only thou, but also all that hear me this day, were both almost, and altogether such as I am, except these bonds.

chapters.zh: 于是，王和巡抚并百尼基与同坐的人都起来，
chapters.en: And when he had thus spoken, the king rose up, and the governor, and Bernice, and they that sat with them:

chapters.zh: 退到里面，彼此谈论说：这人并没有犯甚么该死该绑的罪。
chapters.en: And when they were gone aside, they talked between themselves, saying, This man doeth nothing worthy of death or of bonds.

chapters.zh: 亚基帕又对非斯都说：这人若没有上告于该撒，就可以释放了。
chapters.en: Then said Agrippa unto Festus, This man might have been set at liberty, if he had not appealed unto Caesar.

chapters.zh: 非斯都既然定规了，叫我们坐船往义大利去，便将保罗和别的囚犯交给御营里的一个百夫长，名叫犹流。
chapters.en: And when it was determined that we should sail into Italy, they delivered Paul and certain other prisoners unto named Julius, a centurion of Augustus' band.

chapters.zh: 有一只亚大米田的船，要沿著亚西亚一带地方的海边走，我们就上了那船开行；有马其顿的帖撒罗尼迦人亚里达古和我们同去。
chapters.en: And entering into a ship of Adramyttium, we launched, meaning to sail by the coasts of Asia; Aristarchus, a Macedonian of Thessalonica, being with us.

chapters.zh: 第二天，到了西顿；犹流宽待保罗，准他往朋友那里去，受他们的照应。
chapters.en: And the next we touched at Sidon. And Julius courteously entreated Paul, and gave liberty to go unto his friends to refresh himself.

chapters.zh: 从那里又开船，因为风不顺，就贴著居比路背风岸行去。
chapters.en: And when we had launched from thence, we sailed under Cyprus, because the winds were contrary.

chapters.zh: 过了基利家、旁非利亚前面的海，就到了吕家的每拉。
chapters.en: And when we had sailed over the sea of Cilicia and Pamphylia, we came to Myra, of Lycia.

chapters.zh: 在那里，百夫长遇见一只亚力山大的船，要往义大利去，便叫我们上了那船。
chapters.en: And there the centurion found a ship of Alexandria sailing into Italy; and he put us therein.

chapters.zh: 一连多日，船行得慢，仅仅来到革尼土的对面。因为被风拦阻，就贴著革哩底背风岸，从撒摩尼对面行过。
chapters.en: And when we had sailed slowly many days, and scarce were come over against Cnidus, the wind not suffering us, we sailed under Crete, over against Salmone;

chapters.zh: 我们沿岸行走，仅仅来到一个地方，名叫佳澳；离那里不远，有拉西亚城。
chapters.en: And, hardly passing it, came unto a place which is called The fair havens; nigh whereunto was the city Lasea.

chapters.zh: 走的日子多了，已经过了禁食的节期，行船又危险，保罗就劝众人说：
chapters.en: Now when much time was spent, and when sailing was now dangerous, because the fast was now already past, Paul admonished ,

chapters.zh: 众位，我看这次行船，不但货物和船要受伤损，大遭破坏，连我们的性命也难保。
chapters.en: And said unto them, Sirs, I perceive that this voyage will be with hurt and much damage, not only of the lading and ship, but also of our lives.

chapters.zh: 但百夫长信从掌船的和船主，不信从保罗所说的。
chapters.en: Nevertheless the centurion believed the master and the owner of the ship, more than those things which were spoken by Paul.

chapters.zh: 且因在这海口过冬不便，船上的人就多半说：不如开船离开这地方，或者能到非尼基过冬。非尼基是革哩底的一个海口，一面朝东北，一面朝东南。
chapters.en: And because the haven was not commodious to winter in, the more part advised to depart thence also, if by any means they might attain to Phenice, to winter; an haven of Crete, and lieth toward the south west and north west.

chapters.zh: 这时，微微起了南风，他们以为得意，就起了锚，贴近革哩底行去。
chapters.en: And when the south wind blew softly, supposing that they had obtained purpose, loosing , they sailed close by Crete.

chapters.zh: 不多几时，狂风从岛上扑下来；那风名叫友拉革罗。
chapters.en: But not long after there arose against it a tempestuous wind, called Euroclydon.

chapters.zh: 船被风抓住，敌不住风，我们就任风刮去。
chapters.en: And when the ship was caught, and could not bear up into the wind, we let drive.

chapters.zh: 贴著一个小岛的背风岸奔行，那岛名叫高大，在那里仅仅收住了小船。
chapters.en: And running under a certain island which is called Clauda, we had much work to come by the boat:

chapters.zh: 既然把小船拉上来，就用缆索捆绑船底，又恐怕在赛耳底沙滩上搁了浅，就落下篷来，任船飘去。
chapters.en: Which when they had taken up, they used helps, undergirding the ship; and, fearing lest they should fall into the quicksands, strake sail, and so were driven.

chapters.zh: 我们被风浪逼得甚急，第二天众人就把货物抛在海里。
chapters.en: And we being exceedingly tossed with a tempest, the next they lightened the ship;

chapters.zh: 到第三天，他们又亲手把船上的器具抛弃了。
chapters.en: And the third we cast out with our own hands the tackling of the ship.

chapters.zh: 太阳和星辰多日不显露，又有狂风大浪催逼，我们得救的指望就都绝了。
chapters.en: And when neither sun nor stars in many days appeared, and no small tempest lay on , all hope that we should be saved was then taken away.

chapters.zh: 众人多日没有吃甚么，保罗就出来站在他们中间，说：众位，你们本该听我的话，不离开革哩底，免得遭这样的伤损破坏。
chapters.en: But after long abstinence Paul stood forth in the midst of them, and said, Sirs, ye should have hearkened unto me, and not have loosed from Crete, and to have gained this harm and loss.

chapters.zh: 现在我还劝你们放心，你们的性命一个也不失丧，惟独失丧这船。
chapters.en: And now I exhort you to be of good cheer: for there shall be no loss of life among you, but of the ship.

chapters.zh: 因我所属所事奉的神，他的使者昨夜站在我旁边，说：
chapters.en: For there stood by me this night the angel of God, whose I am, and whom I serve,

chapters.zh: 保罗，不要害怕，你必定站在该撒面前，并且与你同船的人，神都赐给你了。
chapters.en: Saying, Fear not, Paul; thou must be brought before Caesar: and, lo, God hath given thee all them that sail with thee.

chapters.zh: 所以众位可以放心，我信神他怎样对我说：事情也要怎样成就。
chapters.en: Wherefore, sirs, be of good cheer: for I believe God, that it shall be even as it was told me.

chapters.zh: 只是我们必要撞在一个岛上。
chapters.en: Howbeit we must be cast upon a certain island.

chapters.zh: 到了第十四天夜间，船在亚底亚海飘来飘去。约到半夜，水手以为渐近旱地，
chapters.en: But when the fourteenth night was come, as we were driven up and down in Adria, about midnight the shipmen deemed that they drew near to some country;

chapters.zh: 就探深浅，探得有十二丈；稍往前行，又探深浅，探得有九丈。
chapters.en: And sounded, and found twenty fathoms: and when they had gone a little further, they sounded again, and found fifteen fathoms.

chapters.zh: 恐怕撞在石头上，就从船尾抛下四个锚，盼望天亮。
chapters.en: Then fearing lest we should have fallen upon rocks, they cast four anchors out of the stern, and wished for the day.

chapters.zh: 水手想要逃出船去，把小船放在海里，假作要从船头抛锚的样子。
chapters.en: And as the shipmen were about to flee out of the ship, when they had let down the boat into the sea, under colour as though they would have cast anchors out of the foreship,

chapters.zh: 保罗对百夫长和兵丁说：这些人若不等在船上，你们必不能得救。
chapters.en: Paul said to the centurion and to the soldiers, Except these abide in the ship, ye cannot be saved.

chapters.zh: 于是兵丁砍断小船的绳子，由他飘去。
chapters.en: Then the soldiers cut off the ropes of the boat, and let her fall off.

chapters.zh: 天渐亮的时候，保罗劝众人都吃饭，说：你们悬望忍饿不吃甚么，已经十四天了。
chapters.en: And while the day was coming on, Paul besought all to take meat, saying, This day is the fourteenth day that ye have tarried and continued fasting, having taken nothing.

chapters.zh: 所以我劝你们吃饭，这是关乎你们救命的事；因为你们各人连一根头发也不至于损坏。
chapters.en: Wherefore I pray you to take meat: for this is for your health: for there shall not an hair fall from the head of any of you.

chapters.zh: 保罗说了这话，就拿著饼，在众人面前祝谢了神，擘开吃。
chapters.en: And when he had thus spoken, he took bread, and gave thanks to God in presence of them all: and when he had broken , he began to eat.

chapters.zh: 于是他们都放下心，也就吃了。
chapters.en: Then were they all of good cheer, and they also took meat.

chapters.zh: 我们在船上的共有二百七十六个人。
chapters.en: And we were in all in the ship two hundred threescore and sixteen souls.

chapters.zh: 他们吃饱了，就把船上的麦子抛在海里，为要叫船轻一点。
chapters.en: And when they had eaten enough, they lightened the ship, and cast out the wheat into the sea.

chapters.zh: 到了天亮，他们不认识那地方，但见一个海湾，有岸可登，就商议能把船拢进去不能。
chapters.en: And when it was day, they knew not the land: but they discovered a certain creek with a shore, into the which they were minded, if it were possible, to thrust in the ship.

chapters.zh: 于是砍断缆索，弃锚在海里；同时也松开舵绳，拉起头篷，顺著风向岸行去。
chapters.en: And when they had taken up the anchors, they committed unto the sea, and loosed the rudder bands, and hoised up the mainsail to the wind, and made toward shore.

chapters.zh: 但遇著两水夹流的地方，就把船搁了浅；船头胶住不动，船尾被浪的猛力冲坏。
chapters.en: And falling into a place where two seas met, they ran the ship aground; and the forepart stuck fast, and remained unmoveable, but the hinder part was broken with the violence of the waves.

chapters.zh: 兵丁的意思要把囚犯杀了，恐怕有洑水脱逃的。
chapters.en: And the soldiers' counsel was to kill the prisoners, lest any of them should swim out, and escape.

chapters.zh: 但百夫长要救保罗，不准他们任意而行，就吩咐会洑水的，跳下水去先上岸；
chapters.en: But the centurion, willing to save Paul, kept them from purpose; and commanded that they which could swim should cast first , and get to land:

chapters.zh: 其余的人可以用板子或船上的零碎东西上岸。这样，众人都得了救，上了岸。
chapters.en: And the rest, some on boards, and some on of the ship. And so it came to pass, that they escaped all safe to land.

chapters.zh: 我们既已得救，才知道那岛名叫米利大。
chapters.en: And when they were escaped, then they knew that the island was called Melita.

chapters.zh: 土人看待我们，有非常的情分；因为当时下雨，天气又冷，就生火接待我们众人。
chapters.en: And the barbarous people shewed us no little kindness: for they kindled a fire, and received us every one, because of the present rain, and because of the cold.

chapters.zh: 那时，保罗拾起一捆柴，放在火上，有一条毒蛇，因为热了出来，咬住他的手。
chapters.en: And when Paul had gathered a bundle of sticks, and laid on the fire, there came a viper out of the heat, and fastened on his hand.

chapters.zh: 土人看见那毒蛇悬在他手上，就彼此说：这人必是个凶手，虽然从海里救上来，天理还不容他活著。
chapters.en: And when the barbarians saw the beast hang on his hand, they said among themselves, No doubt this man is a murderer, whom, though he hath escaped the sea, yet vengeance suffereth not to live.

chapters.zh: 保罗竟把那毒蛇甩在火里，并没有受伤。
chapters.en: And he shook off the beast into the fire, and felt no harm.

chapters.zh: 土人想他必要肿起来，或是忽然仆倒死了；看了多时，见他无害，就转念，说：他是个神。
chapters.en: Howbeit they looked when he should have swollen, or fallen down dead suddenly: but after they had looked a great while, and saw no harm come to him, they changed their minds, and said that he was a god.

chapters.zh: 离那地方不远，有田产是岛长部百流的；他接纳我们，尽情款待三日。
chapters.en: In the same quarters were possessions of the chief man of the island, whose name was Publius; who received us, and lodged us three days courteously.

chapters.zh: 当时，部百流的父亲患热病和痢疾躺著。保罗进去，为他祷告，按手在他身上，治好了他。
chapters.en: And it came to pass, that the father of Publius lay sick of a fever and of a bloody flux: to whom Paul entered in, and prayed, and laid his hands on him, and healed him.

chapters.zh: 从此，岛上其余的病人也来，得了医治。
chapters.en: So when this was done, others also, which had diseases in the island, came, and were healed:

chapters.zh: 他们又多方的尊敬我们；到了开船的时候，也把我们所需用的送到船上。
chapters.en: Who also honoured us with many honours; and when we departed, they laded with such things as were necessary.

chapters.zh: 过了三个月，我们上了亚力山大的船往前行；这船以丢斯双子为记，是在那海岛过了冬的。
chapters.en: And after three months we departed in a ship of Alexandria, which had wintered in the isle, whose sign was Castor and Pollux.

chapters.zh: 到了叙拉古，我们停泊三日；
chapters.en: And landing at Syracuse, we tarried three days.

chapters.zh: 又从那里绕行，来到利基翁。过了一天，起了南风，第二天就来到部丢利。
chapters.en: And from thence we fetched a compass, and came to Rhegium: and after one day the south wind blew, and we came the next day to Puteoli:

chapters.zh: 在那里遇见弟兄们，请我们与他们同住了七天。这样，我们来到罗马。
chapters.en: Where we found brethren, and were desired to tarry with them seven days: and so we went toward Rome.

chapters.zh: 那里的弟兄们一听见我们的信息就出来，到亚比乌市和三馆地方迎接我们。保罗见了他们，就感谢神，放心壮胆。
chapters.en: And from thence, when the brethren heard of us, they came to meet us as far as Appii forum, and The three taverns: whom when Paul saw, he thanked God, and took courage.

chapters.zh: 进了罗马城，（有古卷在此有：百夫长把众囚犯交给御营的统领，惟有）保罗蒙准和一个看守他的兵另住在一处。
chapters.en: And when we came to Rome, the centurion delivered the prisoners to the captain of the guard: but Paul was suffered to dwell by himself with a soldier that kept him.

chapters.zh: 过了三天，保罗请犹太人的首领来。他们来了，就对他们说：弟兄们，我虽没有做甚么事干犯本国的百姓和我们祖宗的规条，却被锁绑，从耶路撒冷解在罗马人的手里。
chapters.en: And it came to pass, that after three days Paul called the chief of the Jews together: and when they were come together, he said unto them, Men brethren, though I have committed nothing against the people, or customs of our fathers, yet was I delivered prisoner from Jerusalem into the hands of the Romans.

chapters.zh: 他们审问了我，就愿意释放我；因为在我身上，并没有该死的罪。罪。
chapters.en: Who, when they had examined me, would have let go, because there was no cause of death in me.

chapters.zh: 无奈犹太人不服，我不得已，只好上告于该撒，并非有甚么事要控告我本国的百姓。
chapters.en: But when the Jews spake against , I was constrained to appeal unto Caesar; not that I had ought to accuse my nation of.

chapters.zh: 因此，我请你们来见面说话，我原为以色列人所指望的，被这炼子捆锁。
chapters.en: For this cause therefore have I called for you, to see , and to speak with : because that for the hope of Israel I am bound with this chain.

chapters.zh: 他们说：我们并没有接著从犹太来论你的信，也没有弟兄到这里来报给我们说你有甚么不好处。
chapters.en: And they said unto him, We neither received letters out of Judaea concerning thee, neither any of the brethren that came shewed or spake any harm of thee.

chapters.zh: 但我们愿意听你的意见如何；因为这教门，我们晓得是到处被毁谤的。
chapters.en: But we desire to hear of thee what thou thinkest: for as concerning this sect, we know that every where it is spoken against.

chapters.zh: 他们和保罗约定了日子，就有许多人到他的寓处来。保罗从早到晚，对他们讲论这事，证明神国的道，引摩西的律法和先知的书，以耶稣的事劝勉他们。
chapters.en: And when they had appointed him a day, there came many to him into lodging; to whom he expounded and testified the kingdom of God, persuading them concerning Jesus, both out of the law of Moses, and the prophets, from morning till evening.

chapters.zh: 他所说的话，有信的，有不信的。
chapters.en: And some believed the things which were spoken, and some believed not.

chapters.zh: 他们彼此不合，就散了；未散以先，保罗说了一句话，说：圣灵藉先知以赛亚向你们祖宗所说的话是不错的。
chapters.en: And when they agreed not among themselves, they departed, after that Paul had spoken one word, Well spake the Holy Ghost by Esaias the prophet unto our fathers,

chapters.zh: 他说：你去告诉这百姓说：你们听是要听见，却不明白；看是要看见，却不晓得；
chapters.en: Saying, Go unto this people, and say, Hearing ye shall hear, and shall not understand; and seeing ye shall see, and not perceive:

chapters.zh: 因为这百姓油蒙了心，耳朵发沉，眼睛闭著；恐怕眼睛看见，耳朵听见，心里明白，回转过来，我就医治他们。
chapters.en: For the heart of this people is waxed gross, and their ears are dull of hearing, and their eyes have they closed; lest they should see with eyes, and hear with ears, and understand with heart, and should be converted, and I should heal them.

chapters.zh: 所以你们当知道，神这救恩，如今传给外邦人，他们也必听受。（有古卷在此有：
chapters.en: Be it known therefore unto you, that the salvation of God is sent unto the Gentiles, and they will hear it.

chapters.zh: 保罗说了这话，犹太人议论纷纷的就走了。）
chapters.en: And when he had said these words, the Jews departed, and had great reasoning among themselves.

chapters.zh: 保罗在自己所租的房子里住了足足两年。凡来见他的人，他全都接待，
chapters.en: And Paul dwelt two whole years in his own hired house, and received all that came in unto him,

chapters.zh: 放胆传讲神国的道，将主耶稣基督的事教导人，并没有人禁止。
chapters.en: Preaching the kingdom of God, and teaching those things which concern the Lord Jesus Christ, with all confidence, no man forbidding him.
